fields.description:
# S6-pre ã GitHub mirror-lock pre-work (snapshot, ADR, backend/token scope)

## Why
The GitHub mirror lock (S6) is a one-way-door change to a PUBLIC repo. De-risk it by doing
the no-live-repo-change pre-work FIRST: capture a restorable snapshot of the current
protection, record the decision + accepted risk in an ADR, and confirm the IaC backend +
token scope ã so S6's live apply is fast, ordered, and reversible. This story makes NO change
to the live repo.

## What
- Snapshot the existing `main-protection` ruleset (DERISK E5: id 18048287 ã `pull_request` +
  `required_status_checks` + `deletion` + `non_fast_forward`, admins bypass-via-PR) to a
  committed `infra/github/main-protection.snapshot.json`
  (`gh api /repos/navapbc/rebar/rulesets/18048287`), so the prior PR-based protection can be
  restored on rollback. Snapshot ONLY ã the live ruleset is not modified here.
- Write `docs/adr/0011-github-mirror-lock.md`: the repo-level-vs-org-level trade-off (free org
  -> no admin-proof org-level rule; trusted-admin editability accepted), the DeployKey-bypass
  `gh api` workaround (UI omits DeployKey; terraform-provider-github historically rejects it) +
  its retirement path, and the one-way-door nature (replaces the human-PR model; disables
  PRs/Issues/Actions).
- Confirm the `Administration: write` token scope (fine-grained PAT scoped to navapbc/rebar,
  injected as a `sensitive` Terraform var from a CI secret, never in `.tfvars`) and the shared
  remote backend + state locking that S6 will use.

## Scope
In: snapshot + ADR + token-scope/backend confirmation (NO live-repo changes). Out: creating /
enforcing the rulesets + mirror hygiene (S6); the Gerrit-side replication (S5).

## Files
- `infra/github/main-protection.snapshot.json` (committed snapshot, for rollback)
- `docs/adr/0011-github-mirror-lock.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `infra/github/main-protection.snapshot.json` contains the full current definition of
      ruleset 18048287 (captured via `gh api`), sufficient to recreate it on rollback.
- [ ] `docs/adr/0011-github-mirror-lock.md` records the repo-level-vs-org-level trade-off, the
      accepted trusted-admin-editability risk, the DeployKey `gh api` bypass workaround +
      retirement path, and the one-way-door nature.
- [ ] The required token scope (`Administration: write`, fine-grained PAT scoped to this repo,
      injected as a `sensitive` var from a CI secret, never committed) is documented.
- [ ] The shared remote backend + state locking that S6 will use is confirmed available; this
      story makes NO change to the live repo (snapshot/docs only).
